messages.content:
Read the text. 
We all know how it feels to get angry. Sometimes anger is mild, when you're just irritated with someone. But at other times anger can be more extreme, with explosive rages. You start to shout and throw things around. You lose control. Your blood pressure increases, your heart races and you can't think about anything else. But what exactly is anger?

Anger is a normal response to a situation where you feel you are being attacked, treated unfairly, let down by others, hurt or rejected. Anger can help you to defend yourself, deal with the problem and let others know how you feel. But anger can also cause problems. If you always lose control, people might start to avoid you. Friends and colleagues may be afraid of your temper and leave you alone. Getting angry might make you feel better; giving you energy and making you feel like you're in control. But it might also leave you feeling hurt and misunderstood. Also, getting angry doesn't usually solve the problem.

1	Work out what makes you angry-You need to understand what situations make you angry. Is it when people make comments about your work or criticise you? Is it that you get angry when you're frustrated and can't do the things you want? Do you get angry when you're tired? Understanding what makes you angry can help you to deal with the problem.

2	Understand the signals - Learn to identify the signs that you're getting angry. Do you start to shout and feel tense? Do you pace about the room? You need to recognise your own signs before you can change your behaviour.

3	Take control of your mind and body-Try taking a step back and distancing yourself from the situation, both physically and emotionally. Check your breathing and take some deep breaths to slow you down and calm your heartbeat. Ask yourself,'Am I overreacting?',’Am I listening to what people are saying?1 Can you find a way to be assertive, rather than aggressive? Try to speak calmly, rather than shout. Try the'Is it worth it?' test. Ask yourself, 'Will it really matter in one month, one year?' Do some physical exercise, like going for a run or a swim. Or try some meditation or listen to music. Do whatever helps to calm you down.

Complete the statements with information from the article.

Try to speak [answer] rather than shout.
Try to speak calmly rather than shout.

Complete the statements with information from the article.

When you get angry, your blood pressure [answer] and your heart speeds up.
When you get angry, your blood pressure increases and your heart races.

Choose the correct variant.

We [answer] very surprised if they don’t win the competition.
We will be very surprised if they don’t win the competition.

Choose the correct variant.

When people [answer] their jobs, they often worry about finding a new one.
When people lose their jobs, they often worry about finding a new one.

Choose the correct variant.

When people (will lose / lose) their jobs, they often worry about finding a new one.
When people lose their jobs, they often worry about finding a new one.

Choose the correct variant.

What (do / will) you usually do when you’re frightened?
What do you usually do when you’re frightened?

Choose the correct variant.

If I ('m / 'll be) happy, I usually laugh a lot and try and make other people laugh too.
If I'm happy, I usually laugh a lot and try and make other people laugh too.

Choose the correct variant.

When I (feel / willl feel) tired, I usually take a long bath to relax.
When I feel tired, I usually take a long bath to relax.

Write out the word which is NOT needed.

If Simon did lost some weight, he could fit into his favourite suit.
If Simon did lose some weight, he could fit into his favourite suit. (The word "did" is not needed).

Write out the word which is NOT needed.

What would you do if you will won a million dollars on the lottery?
What would you do if you won a million dollars on the lottery? (The word "will" is not needed).

Write out the word which is NOT needed.

If my computer didn't crashed, I’d call a friend and ask him to help fix it.
If my computer didn't crash, I’d call a friend and ask him to help fix it. (The word "did" is not needed).

Give the word to the definition.

the type of software that lets you design web pages without writing HTML codes
The word is "WYSIWYG".

Give the word to the definition.

a mechanism for adding styles to web documents
The word is "CSS".

Give the word to the definition.

a method of displaying multiple HTML documents in the same browser window
The word is "frames".

Complete the sentences with the correct form of the words in the box. 

(anser: annoy     confuse     embarrass     relax     satisfy     worry)
 

It was really [anser] when I introduced my boss to everyone at the meeting but got his name wrong.
It was really embarrassing when I introduced my boss to everyone at the meeting but got his name wrong.

Complete the sentences with the correct form of the words in the box.

(anser: annoy confuse embarrass relax satisfy worry)


Our art teacher said she was [anser] with the paintings we had done during our summer holidays as they were really good.
Our art teacher said she was satisfied with the paintings we had done during our summer holidays as they were really good.

Choose the correct preposition.

If you click [anser] the icon there, you’ll be able to download the document you want.
If you click on the icon there, you’ll be able to download the document you want.

Choose the correct preposition.

Tania and Kate get [anser] really well and have been best friends since school.
Tania and Kate get on really well and have been best friends since school.

Choose the correct preposition.

Please take [anser] your shoes and leave them by the door
Please take off your shoes and leave them by the door.

Complete the sentence.

(anser: course  tone  promoted  making  doing  place  split  give)

When were we training to become police officers, we were told how to give bad news without [anser] people too upset.
When we were training to become police officers, we were told how to give bad news without making people too upset.

Complete the sentence.

(anser: course  tone  promoted  making  doing  place  split  give)

We heard that the company was moving its offices abroad but our boss couldn’t [anser] any reasons why.
We heard that the company was moving its offices abroad but our boss couldn’t give any reasons why.

Complete the sentence.

(anser: course  tone  promoted  making  doing  place  split  give)

When Pauline told us she was getting [anser] to area manager, we were all really happy for her.
When Pauline told us she was getting promoted to area manager, we were all really happy for her.

Complete the sentences with the correct form of the words in the box.

(anser: annoy confuse embarrass relax satisfy worry)


Some people believe that we shouldn’t be so [anser] about climate change. They don’t think it’s really happening.
Some people believe that we shouldn’t be so worried about climate change. They don’t think it’s really happening.
